authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines student-record access and disclosure prerequisites that govern enrollment intake, identity-sharing, and consent behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Explains role-based disclosure and transfer conditions used before completing enrollment routing outcomes.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines protections for human-subject and study-participant workflows that affect enrollment and research-linked intake authority.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal guidance for IRB and consent interpretation that determines when study-related intake requests must be escalated.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines grant stewardship and documentation requirements relevant to grant-connected enrollment and study operations.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides canonical education program/enrollment terminology needed to map intake and placement records consistently.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard for learner-course enrollment schema used for intake record interoperability and handoff consistency.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets public accreditation governance context used to detect when quality/oversight escalation is required before proceeding.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and identity-authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requestor role, student identity, residency, enrollment access, and authorization signals before any downstream workflow is accepted.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Program, study, and grant prerequisite gating
workflow_stages.description: Validate program-fit, study protocol, grant requirements, and placement constraints before transition readiness is set.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Evidence, assessment, and consent linkage
workflow_stages.description: Confirm FERPA release and assessment support artifacts are present before routing decisions are produced.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Systems dependency and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Verify SIS/LMS/scheduling/research-administration/document system status before marking intake as ready for execution transfer.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and ethics/approval change handling
workflow_stages.description: Route unresolved or changing ethics, consent, or approval states to adjacent owners without finalizing downstream decisions.

allowed: Validate intake readiness, authorization state, prerequisite completeness, and evidence sufficiency.
Route intake to correct adjacent owners with explicit blocker notes.
Escalate ethics, accreditation, or approval-state changes instead of finalizing outcomes.
escalate: Missing consent, records, or assessment evidence in scope
Program or study approval owners are unresolved
Source conflicts or changing ethics/IRB states materially alter routing
refuse: Any grading, instruction, or ethics-board final decisions requested at this lane
Closure or acceptance without completed pre-execution prerequisites

domain_failure_modes: Missing FERPA/consent artifacts while processing learner or study-related intake.
Accepting requests without documented approval ownership for placement, chair, or grant dependencies.
Treating conflicting tenant and public policy rules as non-blocking when they materially affect outcomes.
Resolving ethics or review-state shifts without escalation to the correct reviewer.
Misclassifying grading/instructional requests as in-scope intake actions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Case routed to wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA release precedence when SIS/LMS and research-admin systems disagree on current consent status
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Treat as runtime ambiguity; require explicit tenant precedence artifact before routing to execution owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accreditation-linked review timing vs admission-cycle urgency
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: When uncertain, preserve queued state and escalate to accrediting-advice owner before completion.

notes: Source mix intentionally spans policy, workflow, evidence, and terminology domains to avoid generic intake behavior.
No retained-implementation authority is claimed; runtime remains `specification-based` pending explicit governance promotion.
